heading: Sexual Assault Advisory Council
label: 51327 Sexual Assault Advisory Council

children.heading: .—Establishment
children.label: a .—Establishment
children.text: The Secretary of Transportation shall establish a Sexual Assault Advisory Council (in this section referred to as the “Council”).

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Council shall be composed of not fewer than 8 and not more than 14 individuals selected by the Secretary of Transportation who are alumni that have graduated within the last 4 years or current midshipmen of the United States Merchant Marine Academy (including midshipmen or alumni who were victims of sexual assault, to the maximum extent practicable, and midshipmen or alumni who were not victims of sexual assault) and governmental and nongovernmental experts and professionals in the sexual assault field.

children.label: A
children.text: not less than 1 member who is licensed in the field of mental health and has prior experience working as a counselor or therapist providing mental health care to survivors of sexual assault in a victim services agency or organization; and

children.label: B
children.text: not less than 1 member who has prior experience developing or implementing sexual assault or sexual harassment prevention and response policies in an academic setting.

children.heading: .—Rules regarding membership
children.label: 3 .—Rules regarding membership
children.text: No employee of the Department of Transportation shall be a member of the Council. The number of governmental experts appointed to the Council shall not exceed the number of nongovernmental experts.

children.label: i
children.text: the policies on sexual harassment, dating violence, domestic violence, sexual assault, and stalking under ;section 51318 of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: the trends and patterns of data contained in the system described under ; andsection 51325 of this title

children.label: iii
children.text: related matters the Council views as appropriate; and

children.label: B
children.text: develop recommendations designed to ensure that such policies and such matters conform, to the extent practicable, to best practices in the field of sexual assault and sexual harassment response and prevention.

children.label: A
children.text: interview current and former midshipmen of the United States Merchant Marine Academy (to the extent that such midshipmen provide the Department of Transportation express consent to be interviewed by the Council); and

children.label: B
children.text: review surveys under section 51322(d).1See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: .—Personally identifiable information
children.label: 3 .—Personally identifiable information
children.text: In carrying out this subsection, the Council shall comply with the obligations of the Department of Transportation to protect personally identifiable information.

children.heading: .—Reports
children.label: d .—Reports
children.text: On an annual basis for each of the 5 years after the date of enactment of this section, and at the discretion of the Council thereafter, the Council shall submit, to the President and the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation and the Committee on Appropriations of the Senate and the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure and the Committee on Appropriations of the House of Representatives, a report on the Council’s findings based on the reviews conducted pursuant to subsection (c) and related recommendations.

children.heading: .—Employee Status
children.label: e .—Employee Status
children.text: Members of the Council shall not be considered employees of the United States Government for any purpose and shall not receive compensation other than reimbursement of travel expenses and per diem allowance in accordance with .section 5703 of title 5

children.heading: .—Nonapplicability of FACA
children.label: f .—Nonapplicability of FACA
children.text: The Federal Advisory Committee Act (5 U.S.C. App.) shall not apply to the Council.1
